Module 1 · Core

Market Saturation Index

Find neighborhoods with the strongest unmet demand for high-quality care
  • Pinpoint white space: Identify neighborhoods where Tier 4/5 demand outstrips current supply — surfacing greenfield and de novo opportunities
  • De-risk site selection: Avoid overbuilt zones and validate new locations against household income, child population, and competitor density
  • Sharpen M&A targeting: Prioritize acquisition targets in underserved zones; screen out those facing structural oversupply
  • Defend the portfolio: Flag existing schools facing rising local saturation so pricing and marketing decisions can be made proactively
  • Calibrated to ECE reality: Built on Tuscany's tiered provider database and proprietary demand model — not generic retail trade area heuristics

Baltimore MSA — Saturation Index

Tier 4/5 · Supply vs. Demand

Estimated High-HHI Under-5 Pop: 22,000  ·  Tier 4/5 Centers: 77

Tier 4 & Tier 5 split
ZoneDemandT4 SupplyT5 SupplyStatus

Towson9.121Underserved

Dundalk4.295Saturated
Pikesville ★9.610Strong Opp.

Demand score 1–10. Higher = stronger unmet demand relative to current supply. Plotting Tier 4/5 providers alone does not show where opportunity is strongest — the index adjusts for local high-income household density.

Module 2 · Core

50 State Universal Pre-K Tracker

See where state policy creates tailwinds and headwinds for private operators
  • Anticipate policy tailwinds/headwinds: See where expanding public Pre-K supports private growth vs. threatens 4-year-old enrollment
  • Prioritize states for expansion: Rank target states by funding, eligibility, mixed-delivery rules, and quality standards that favor private providers
  • Unlock public funding streams: Identify states where private centers can participate in Pre-K contracts, vouchers, or subsidies
  • Stress-test the portfolio: Flag schools in states moving toward universal public Pre-K so pricing and capacity can be repositioned in advance
Key Feature
Drill-down from state to MSA level · State-by-state UPK policy status · Enrollment & funding changes over time · Private provider participation requirements · Real-time newsfeed of proposed legislation

CO UPK 2026–27 Rates — Colorado published updated reimbursement rates for community-based providers. Rates increased 8% YoY — net positive for private operators in Colorado.
June 20, 2026
CA Budget Cuts — California proposed $340M reduction to TK expansion. Enrollment targets for 2026–27 may not be met, reducing competitive pressure from TK on Tier 4/5 in Southern CA.
June 18, 2026
GA Pre-K Voucher Study — Legislature considering pilot for Pre-K vouchers redeemable at Tier 4/5 providers. Final vote expected September 2026.
June 12, 2026
Module 3 · Core

Online Sentiment Monitoring

Always-on visibility into what parents are saying about you and your competitors
  • Catch issues before they spread: Surface negative sentiment shifts at individual schools in time to intervene, not after enrollment drops
  • Know what parents really value: Categorical themes reveal which drivers — staff, communication, curriculum, facilities — are moving the conversation
  • Watch competitors in real time: Track named competitors' sentiment trends to spot weakness to exploit or strengths to learn from
  • Strengthen diligence and reviews: Bring sentiment data into acquisition evaluations and post-close performance reviews of school directors

Sources: Google · Facebook · Winnie · Care.com  ·  Pricing: $45/center/month with volume discounts to $20/center/month at 500+ centers

Add-On Module

Competitive Performance Assessment

See exactly how you stack up against local competitors on price and quality
  • See yourself as parents see you: Benchmark your schools against local competitors on the price and quality factors families actually use to decide
  • Optimize pricing power: Identify schools underpriced relative to their competitive position — and those at risk of being overpriced
  • Target investment where it pays back: Pinpoint quality gaps — curriculum, facilities, staff, hours — that most threaten enrollment in each market
  • Sharpen acquisition diligence: Pressure-test target schools' competitive standing before underwriting growth assumptions
  • Arm the field team: Give directors a clear, evidence-based view of how to position against each named competitor on tours and inquiries

Add-On Module

Managed Mystery Shopping

Minimize internal team time and stress — get accurate data and insights you can trust
  • Time Savings: Our team handles all logistics so you can focus on staff and students
  • Anonymity: Client information is never shared with shoppers
  • Professional Shoppers: Two rounds of training; shoppers must meet qualifying criteria (e.g., ECE parent age)
  • Quality Assurance: Reports undergo two rounds of QA; shops repeated at no cost if not up to standard

Key Features

  • Curated, on-the-ground intelligence on how client's and competitors' schools present during inquiry and tour
  • Findings delivered through a bespoke client view with summary scoring, narrative observations, and supporting artifacts
  • Cadence and scope defined per engagement; managed by Tuscany staff
How It Works
Provide Tuscany with a list of your centers. Tuscany can also identify competitors or use your competitor list. Duration typically takes 5+ weeks depending on the number of centers. Final deliverables include mystery shopping reports for each location and a consultative session to review findings.

Sample Shop Report — Colleyville E (In-Person Tour)

June 10, 2026 · QA: Pass
CategoryScoreComp. AvgObservationAction
Entrance & GroundsAB+Well-maintained; branded signage clearNo action needed
Welcome & ResponsivenessBB+Director greeting took 8 min; should be <2 minDirector coaching needed
Classroom QualityA−BStrong teacher-student ratios; curriculum visibleHighlight in marketing
Outdoor FacilitiesAB−Best-in-class playground; shopper noted spontaneouslyCompetitive advantage
Price / Waitlist DisclosureC+BDirector deflected pricing twice; no written quote providedTrain director on pricing script
Follow-UpB+BEmail follow-up received within 2 hrs; personalizedBest practice to share

*All questions asked in mystery shop interactions but providers at times withhold some information. Shop was repeated at no cost (first attempt: shopper did not qualify as ECE-parent age).

Add-On Module

Customer Sentiment Study

Hear directly from families what drives enrollment, retention, and referral
  • Identify drivers of enrollment: Identify the specific drivers that turn inquiries into enrolled families
  • Identify drivers of retention: Pinpoint why families stay or leave so directors can act on what actually drives churn
  • Sharper marketing and pricing: Ground messaging, tour scripts, and price decisions in what families value most
  • Track progress over time: Repeatable methodology lets you measure whether changes are moving sentiment
Methodology
Qualitative & Quantitative Jobs-To-Be-Done (Ulwick 1990, popularized by Clay Christensen). Bespoke client view with findings, segmentation, and recommended actions. Reproducible so studies can be repeated for trending.

Use Cases

Tuscany's integrated Early Care Data Platform is applicable to many use cases

De Novo Site Selection
Identify the strongest neighborhoods and trade areas for new schools by combining the Tier 4/5 Saturation Index, demographics, and Universal Pre-K policy tailwinds.
New School Pricing Strategy
Set defensible opening prices for new locations using competitor tuition benchmarks by classroom and trade area.
M&A Quality & Price Diligence
Surface acquisition candidates and stress-test targets using competitive positioning, brand sentiment, and price/quality benchmarks against local peers.
School Performance Improvement
Benchmark each school against local competitors on price and quality factors parents weigh most — translating gaps into targeted operational and marketing actions.
Brand Conversion Playbook
Guide the transition of acquisition targets — informing pricing, parent communications, curriculum rollout, and occupancy recovery if needed. Case result: 47% → 84% occupancy over 4 years.
Enrollment Diagnostics
Combine internal utilization data, mystery shop rankings, NPS scores, and demographic analysis to identify potential occupancy pain points site by site.
Market Context

A Few Insights on the Early Care Market

Key structural trends shaping the ECE landscape
18.3M
US Under-5 Population (2024)
−6.4%
Decline from 2019 peak
1.57
US Total Fertility Rate (2025 record low)
72%
Market share held by Tiers 3–5

U.S. Childcare Market — Five Quality/Price Tiers™

Tier 5 · Premium ECE

National/regional · Corporate or franchise · Excel on most quality metrics · Proprietary content & facilities
Primrose, Crème de la Crème, Children's Learning Adventure, Goddard, Bright Horizons

Tier 4 · Upscale ECE

National/regional · Corporate or franchise · All quality metrics met · Premium curriculum & enrichment
Kiddie Academy, The Learning Experience, Lightbridge Academy, Kids 'R' Kids, KinderCare

Tier 3 · Standard ECE

National–local scale · Corporate, franchise, or family-owned · Educational focus differentiates from Tier 2
Knowledge Beginnings, Childtime Learning Center, Local brands

Tiers 3–5 account for 63% of FTE children in paid care and 72% of market size. Market dynamics vary by Quality/Price Tier™ and location. Source: Tuscany Analysis, State Licensing Data.

Key Structural Trends

Under-5 Stabilization: US Census projects the under-five population will remain stable near 18.4–18.5M through 2034, but will not return to pre-pandemic levels of 19.6M.
Record Low Fertility: US Total Fertility Rate hit 1.57 births/woman in 2025, down 19.5% from generational average (1973–2010). Long-run demand headwind for the sector.
Market Consolidation: Large childcare chains grew 14–95% in select states (MN, IA, TN, CT) while family childcare locations declined 4–17%. Higher end of the market is growing even as total enrollment declines.
AI as Differentiator: How you use AI and its time savings will be the primary driver of business strategy. The best brands will use AI to create deeper, meaningful differentiation — Kindergarten Readiness is no longer a differentiator alone.
Enrollment Recovery: Private preschool enrollment rates (3–4 yr olds) returned to pre-pandemic levels in 2022–23. Growth plateau is expected going forward.
About Tuscany

Make high-stakes decisions with confidence

About Tuscany Strategy Consulting

Our Approach

Consulting from an Operator's Mindset. Our partners have extensive experience building multi-million-dollar businesses spanning start-ups to acquisition-driven businesses. We bring a practical, operational approach to growth strategy.

Objectives-Driven Work Practices. Client objectives are the throughline to every workstream, analysis, synthesis, and answer development process. We produce work that is actively used by clients for years, not reports that sit on a shelf.

High Explanatory Power. Tuscany has pioneered proprietary analytics: Quality/Price Tiers™, Price Corridors™, Customer Switching Behavior™, Growth Objectives to KPI Crosswalk, Learn/Use/Teach™, and Qualitative & Quantitative Jobs-To-Be-Done.

Rational Cost Structure. Designed to deliver confidence in approach, data, and insights at considerably lower cost than major competitors — de-risking engagements and making it easier for executives and boards to invest.

Industry Sectors: Early Childhood · Elementary & Secondary · Colleges & Universities · Workforce Development · Special Education · Mental Health & Wellness · Health Services
